title: 10 best herbal teas for pink eye
intro_desc: Herbal teas for pink eye are a natural and soothing remedy for conjunctivitis, an inflammation or infection of the membrane covering the white part of the eye. These teas help to calm the infection, reduce redness and swelling, and promote healing by providing antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Examples of herbal teas that can help include chamomile, peppermint, and calendula, which have been used for centuries to soothe irritated eyes and improve vision clarity. Drinking these teas regularly can significantly alleviate symptoms, allowing individuals to return to their daily activities without the discomfort of pink eye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with pink eye because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's active compounds, such as echinacea and cinnamic acid, work together to reduce redness and swelling, while also combating the bacterial infections that often cause pink eye. As a natural remedy, herbal Kansas coneflower teas may provide quick relief from discomfort and promote healing, making it a popular alternative to traditional medications for this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Kansas coneflower tea contains antimicrobial compounds like triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that help combat bacterial and viral infections causing pink eye.
Anti-inflammatory agents: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and irritation associated with pink eye, making it a natural remedy for soothing the affected eyes.

Antioxidants: Kansas coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and sterols, seeds have natural antibacterial properties that help combat infection-causing bacteria in pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried echinacea angustifolia root and measure out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Combine the echinacea root with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids and reserving the infused liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before using as an eye wash by dipping a clean cloth in the liquid and gently applying it to affected eyes.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with pink eye because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and carotenoids present in the herb have potent antioxidant activities that reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with conjunctivitis. Additionally, pot marigold contains antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacterial infections, which can exacerbate pink eye symptoms. By soothing and calming the affected area, pot marigold teas provide natural relief from redness, itchiness, and discharge, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Pot marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can exacerbate pink eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes associated with pink eye.
Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea's antimicrobial properties may help to combat bacterial or viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease conjunctival irritation.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which aid in combating bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers, drying them immediately to preserve potency for at least an hour.
Grind 10 grams of dried calendula flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Steep the ground calendula in 500ml of boiling water for 15-20 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Use the cooled tea as an eyewash by gently pouring it into the affected eye three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the redness and irritation associated with conjunctivitis. The aloe vera gel found in these teas contains powerful antioxidants that help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial qualities help to combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing pink eye, promoting faster healing and recovery. By consuming aloe tea, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms and reduce the duration of this uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory agents, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye by soothing the inflamed conjunctiva.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds in aloe tea, including saponins and anthraquinones, combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.

Soften and lubricate mucous membranes: Aloe's natural moisturizing properties help soften and lubricate the affected eye, reducing irritation and discomfort, making it easier for the eye to heal and recover from infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain mucilages that provide a protective barrier for the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried aloe vera leaves and add to a large teapot.
Combine the aloe vera leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in the teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes before straining the liquid.
Mix in 1 tablespoon of honey and 1/4 teaspoon of turmeric powder into the tea.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature before applying it to the affected eye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with pink eye because it has potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that effectively combat the underlying infection. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, work to soothe and reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye. Additionally, eyebright's ability to stimulate lymphatic drainage helps to remove toxins and excess fluid from the affected area, promoting faster recovery and reduced risk of recurrence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in eyebright tea, including terpenes and saponins, combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Anti-allergic and anti-irritant properties: Eyebright tea's anti-allergic and anti-irritant compounds help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing itchiness, burning, and discharge associated with pink eye, while also preventing further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them effective in soothing pink eye.
Flowers: Eyebright flowers contain flavonoids that have antibacterial properties, which aid in combating bacterial infections that cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried euphrasia officinalis leaves or flowers.
Measure two cups of water into a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the measured euphrasia officinalis herb to the boiling water.
Pour the strained tea into a clean cup or teapot.
Drink two to three cups of tea throughout the day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea's bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of pink eye. Additionally, the antibacterial and antiviral properties help combat underlying infections, promoting a faster recovery from conjunctivitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can cause eye damage and accelerate the healing process of pink eye.

Antibacterial properties: Dandelion tea's antibacterial compounds, such as caffeic acid and chicoric acid, help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to pink eye symptoms, reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, the roots help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion associated with pink eye.
Flowers: The flowers contain antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested herbs using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air-drying for 24 hours.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale herb and steep it in 1 cup boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids immediately.
Drink the cooled herbal tea three times daily, allowing each serving to sit under your closed eyelids as an eye bath for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as allantoin and plantain saponins, help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the affected eyes. Additionally, plantain's antibacterial and antifungal properties combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting a speedy recovery and reducing the risk of complications. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective treatment for soothing and healing pink eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce swelling and discomfort associated with pink eye, allowing for faster healing.
Antioxidants: Plantain tea is rich in antioxidants, which help combat free radicals that can exacerbate the infection, promoting a faster recovery from pink eye.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in plantain tea help reduce discharge and inflammation, making it an effective remedy for reducing the symptoms of pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them ideal for treating pink eye.
Rhyzomes: Containing astringent properties, they can effectively reduce inflammation and prevent infection from spreading.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidant and antibacterial agents, seeds help combat bacterial and fungal infections associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried plantago major leaves measuring one teaspoon for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a pot then reduce heat to simmer for ten minutes.
Add the dried plantago major leaves to the simmering water and steep for an additional five minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Drink the cooled tea three times daily as needed to soothe pink eye symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with pink eye because its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties provide relief from redness, itching, and swelling associated with this common condition. The tea's ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes also reduces the risk of complications such as conjunctivitis and corneal ulcers. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antibacterial and antiviral agents help combat the underlying infection causing pink eye, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the need for antibiotics or other medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the eyes, promoting healing and reducing irritation.

Mucolytic properties: Nettle tea contains mucolytic compounds like histamine, which can break down mucus and pus in the eyes, helping to clear up congestion and reduce discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce redness in the eyes.
Bark: The bark contains tannins, which aid in reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels, alleviating pink eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and stems from a trusted source, wash them thoroughly with cold running water.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at a low temperature for 6-8 hours or air drying for several weeks.
Combine one teaspoon of dried nettle leaves and stems with one cup of boiling filtered water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the tea mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Drink the cooled herbal tea three times daily, holding it in your eyes for a few seconds before blinking to help with pink eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The soothing properties of chamomile calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial qualities help to combat the bacterial or viral infection that causes pink eye, promoting healing and preventing further spread of the condition. By drinking chamomile tea, you can effectively alleviate symptoms and promote recovery from pink eye, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common eye issue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce swelling and redness in the affected eye, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the eyes from further irritation and damage caused by pink eye, promoting faster recovery.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's natural sedative properties can calm the irritated eyes and surrounding skin, providing a sense of comfort and relief from itchiness, burning, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe redness associated with pink eye.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have antibacterial properties, helping to combat infection and promote healing in cases of pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers or purchase dried chamomile flowers in sufficient quantity for brewing.
Combine one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or three teaspoons of fresh flowers with 200ml boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing the bioactive compounds and flavonoids into the tea.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles or sediment from the brewed liquid.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container and refrigerate for up to 24 hours before drinking, as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the affected area. The tea's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, also possess antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial infections, which are often associated with pink eye. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of licorice tea can provide relief from itching and discomfort, making it a natural and comforting remedy for this common eye condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected eye.

Antimicrobial properties: The bioactive compounds present in licorice root, such as isoflavones and triterpenoids, possess antimicrobial activities that can help combat bacterial and viral infections associated with pink eye, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Soften and lubricate mucous membranes: Licorice tea's demulcent properties can soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing discomfort, inflammation, and the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots are used in tea blends due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated conjunctival tissues.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and promote healing in pink eye cases.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and steep it in one cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean glass cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Use this cooled tea as eye drops by soaking a cotton ball in the liquid and gently applying it to affected eyes.
Repeat the application process every hour or two, up to six times daily, as needed until pink eye symptoms subside.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow teas helps with pink eye because its soothing properties provide relief from inflammation, redness, and itchiness. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the eyes, while its mucilages create a protective barrier on the conjunctiva to calm irritated tissues. Additionally, mallow teas' antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals can experience faster recovery and reduced discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mallow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort and itching.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of mallow tea combat the bacterial or viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
Sothing and protective: Mallow tea's soothing and protective qualities help calm irritated conjunctival tissue, protecting it from further irritation and promoting a healthy environment for recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a soothing substance that helps to reduce inflammation and discharge associated with pink eye.
Leaves: Mallow leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to calm and soothe the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried malva sylvestris flowers in adequate quantities for the desired number of servings.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried malva sylvestris flowers per cup of boiling water and add it to a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of freshly boiled water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a spoon or by squeezing the tea leaves gently against the side of the container.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature and consume immediately, as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pink eye with herbal teas is by using calendula tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling. Chamomile tea soothes the eyes and eases discomfort, while peppermint tea helps to clear congestion. Combine equal parts of each tea, steep for 5-7 minutes, and cool before using as a compress or eyedrop several times a day. Repeat treatment until symptoms subside.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pink eye that are treated with herbal teas are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. Herbal teas such as chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. These teas can be used as a compress or applied directly to the affected area to provide relief from itchiness and inflammation.
